name: Sierra de Tentudía Monastery Loop
name_original: Subida al Monasterio de Tentudía – circular desde Cabeza la Vaca por la Sierra de Tentudía
description: This difficult 39.2-mile mountain biking route climbs 5662 feet to the Monasterio de Tentudía, offering panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cabeza La Vaca offers challenging routes through a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain. The region features prominent peaks and sierras, such as Cerro Bonales and Sierra de Tentudía, providing a mountainous backdrop for rides. Riders can expect to navigate through areas with notable climbs and descents, indicative of a rugged, hilly environment. The trails often lead through natural areas, connecting different geographical features. Best mountain bike trails around Cabeza La Vaca The most popular mountain bike trail is Arroyomolinos de León, Cerro Bonales, Cañaveral de León – ruta circular, a difficult 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 35 minutes to complete. This circular route explores multiple natural features and offers substantial climbing. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Tentudía Monastery Loop, a difficult 39.2 miles (63.0 km) path. This route is the longest listed, featuring significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Tentudía. Local mountain bikers also love the Cerro de Tentudia – Monastery of Tentudía loop from Monesterio, a 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Cabeza La Vaca is defined by challenging climbs, mountain passes, and scenic loops around peaks and sierras. The network offers options for difficult routes, catering to experienced mountain bikers. The routes in Cabeza La Vaca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Nearly 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cabeza La Vaca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: Mountain biking in Cabeza La Vaca is predominantly challenging, characterized by significant elevation changes and rugged terrain. Out of 41 total routes, 21 are classified as difficult and 18 as moderate, catering mostly to experienced riders. There are only 2 easy routes available.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are a couple of easier routes. However, the majority of trails involve substantial climbs and descents, making them more suitable for intermediate to advanced mountain bikers.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032487694/sierra-de-tentudia-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Tentudía Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult path spans 39.2 miles (63.0 km) and features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Tentudía, typically taking around 6 hours 27 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike trails available in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Cabeza La Vaca are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995495135/arroyomolinos-de-leon-cerro-bonales-canaveral-de-leon-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyomolinos de León, Cerro Bonales, Cañaveral de León – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.0-mile loop that explores multiple natural features. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Tentudia – Monastery of Tentudía loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.8-mile trail through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: The trails in Cabeza La Vaca offer panoramic views of prominent peaks and sierras, such as Cerro Bonales and Sierra de Tentudía. Riders can expect scenic vistas of the rugged, hilly environment and natural areas, often leading to high points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonales Summit (1,055 m)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3164075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tentudía Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the mountain bike routes?
A: Along the mountain bike routes, you can encounter several notable attractions. These include natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Cañaveral de León&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Fuentes de León&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, cultural landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4093491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Tentudía&lt;/a&gt; are often part of the routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Nearly 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cabeza La Vaca's varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, mountain passes, and scenic loops around peaks and sierras.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by lakes or water features?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate water features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojales – Laguna de Cañaveral de León loop from Fuentes de León&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Cañaveral de León&lt;/a&gt;, offering a refreshing change of scenery during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: Given the region's mountainous character and significant elevation changes, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder weather to higher elevations, though specific trail conditions vary.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the highest points of the region?
A: Absolutely. Several routes are designed to take you to the region's highest points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995495135/arroyomolinos-de-leon-cerro-bonales-canaveral-de-leon-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyomolinos de León, Cerro Bonales, Cañaveral de León – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; explores Cerro Bonales, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032487694/sierra-de-tentudia-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Tentudía Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt; traverses the Sierra de Tentudía, both offering access to prominent summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonales Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3164075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tentudía Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Cabeza La Vaca?
A: There are 41 mountain bike trails available around Cabeza La Vaca, offering a wide range of options primarily for experienced riders due to the challenging terrain.
tips:
We are in the beautiful natural environment that surrounds the Monastery of Tentudía, where the beautiful Mudejar cloister stands out especially.

The views from the top of Alto de Tentudía are impressive, offering a panoramic view of the region and its landscapes of meadows and mountains typical of the south of Badajoz.

According to legend, in the 13th century during a battle against the Arabs, the master of the Order of Santiago, Pelay Pérez Correa, cried out to the Virgin shouting: "Holy Mary, stop your day" when he saw that night was approaching and the victory was not assured. It is said that the sun stopped on the horizon allowing the Christian troops to win. As thanks, the master ordered the construction of a temple dedicated to Santa María de Tentudía.

At the end of the visit we can go to the bar where we can eat or drink while enjoying the scenery.
Nice area of passage next to the small pond known as La Pantaneta. We will find information panels in the place while we ride along the picturesque path that surrounds it.
Near the Puerto de la Media Legua located at 912 meters above sea level, we find the picturesque and traditional path of Valdezurrones. An ideal space for lovers of mountain biking, gravel or hiking.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route, covering approximately 63 km with over 1700 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 6.5 hours to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views or visit the Monastery of Tentudía.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It features irregular and long ascents, strong descents, and steep ramps that can reach gradients of 14-16%. Portions may be unpaved or cobbled, requiring very good fitness and technical skill. Some sections might even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Subida al Monasterio de Tentudía mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from Cabeza la Vaca, which is the highest town in the province of Badajoz. You should be able to find parking within the town to begin your ride. As it's a circular route, you'll finish back at your starting point.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the picturesque dehesa landscape typical of Extremadura. The route passes through areas abundant with holm oaks, olive groves, chestnut trees, and cork oaks. You'll also see fig trees, vines, rockroses, arbutus, and thyme. As you ascend, you'll be treated to panoramic views over the Sierra de Aracena, culminating in breathtaking 360-degree vistas from the summit of Cerro de Tentudía.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary destination is the historic Monastery of Tentudía, a 13th-century monument atop Cerro de Tentudía, the highest point in Badajoz. You'll also pass by La Pantaneta Pond and ride along sections of the Camino de Valdezarros, offering diverse natural and historical sights.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Tentudía region is best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making the demanding climbs more challenging. Winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions, especially on the unpaved and cobbled sections.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Subida al Monasterio de Tentudía mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Sierra de Tentudía. However, given the challenging nature of this specific mountain bike route, it might not be ideal for all dogs, especially on longer, steeper sections or technical terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are well-hydrated, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to mountain bike in Sierra de Tentudía?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need specific permits or to pay entrance fees to mountain bike this route in the Sierra de Tentudía. It's a protected area of high natural value, so please respect the environment, stay on marked trails, and follow Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ruta de los Ecosistemas Forestales and the Ruta de la Pantaneta, offering glimpses of other established trails in the area.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular (loop) mountain bike route, starting and ending in Cabeza la Vaca. This means you won't have to retrace your steps and can enjoy a continuously changing landscape.

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's crucial to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, and essential bike repair tools (spare tubes, pump, multi-tool). Appropriate mountain biking gear, including a helmet, gloves, and suitable clothing for varying weather conditions, is also highly recommended. A GPS device with the route loaded is advisable due to the varied terrain.
